title: Update to F36 CI VM Images + Testing netavark/aardvark-dns
body:
Update to F36 CI VM Images + Testing netavark/aardvark-dns on F36+

***Depends on:***
* Cirrus: Fix missing git-enforced runtime identity #13956
* Fix using --network-backend on podman-remote #13957
* Workaround criu re-linking output in system test #13958
* Fix hang in apiv2 test_connect #13959
* Fix upgrade tests assuming storage.conf exists #13960

comments.body:
@rhatdan @mheon @vrothberg The F36 VM is failing in validate. Is this a `golang-1.18` thing?  Is there a compatibility-ish[1] flag or some way to disable the eleven-billion `gofmt` changes?

```
************************************************************
Runner executing validate podman-tests as root on fedora-36(fedora-36)
Current environment VM image: fedora-c4789105149083648
************************************************************
find . -name '*.go' -type f \
	-not \( \
		-name '.golangci.yml' -o \
		-name 'Makefile' -o \
		-path './vendor/*' -prune -o \
		-path './contrib/*' -prune \
	\) -exec gofmt -d -e -s -w {} \+
diff -u ./cmd/podman/early_init_unsupported.go.orig ./cmd/podman/early_init_unsupported.go
--- ./cmd/podman/early_init_unsupported.go.orig	2022-02-28 14:13:04.931221238 -0600
+++ ./cmd/podman/early_init_unsupported.go	2022-02-28 14:13:04.931221238 -0600
@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
+//go:build !linux
 // +build !linux
 package main
...cut 10.9 billion more...
```

[1] Note: At a higher-level, this command is coming from the `Makefile`, so we need a solution/fix that's also supported for developers in other environments (down to golang 1.16 IIUC).
It's a Go thing, yes. In order for it to pass, we need to apply all the changes suggested by the linter.

> apply all the changes suggested by the linter

I'm fine with that.  Can you confirm that won't impact folks running `validate` on older golangs we support?

> I'm fine with that. Can you confirm that won't impact folks running `validate` on older golangs we support?

Yes, we're already using that in c/image and probably more parts of the stack.
Great, thanks.  I'll add a dedicated commit for it.

@vrothberg have you seen these golang `1.18` problems WRT `golangci-lint` at all ([some are posted upstream](https://github.com/golangci/golangci-lint/issues/2374))?  If I use `1.36.0` (same as on `main`) I get [these failures](https://github.com/containers/podman/runs/5379081206).  If I update to `1.44.2` (the latest), I get [these failures](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/13376/checks?check_run_id=5381795220) :confused:
Update: I tried `1.42.1` (manually), it doesn't throw errors per say, but reports several screen-fulls of lint violations before `ERRO Timeout exceeded...` :confounded:
foirce-push: Temporarily bypass `validate` requirement for other tasks to run

Well `int podman fedora-36 root host` passed, so that's good.  `int podman fedora-36 root container` [is a complete mess (log)](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5784435122503680).  Hey @mheon @baude, do I need to ensure `$NETWORK_BACKEND=netavark` inside the container?  I think I might be missing that.

All the F35 failures make me worried - it's currently setup for `runc` + `cni`.  Is it time we drop testing of `runc` in Fedora entirely, and just rely on Ubutnu to catch any issues?
I am fine with dropping testing for runc on Fedora as long as we are testing it somewhere.

@cevich, I fear the only way forward is to disable `make lint` on F36 until Go 1.18 has been released and is supported by the linter tool chain.

I feel comfortable skipping it in F36 since we're still running the checks on F35.

> I feel comfortable skipping it in F36 since we're still running the checks on F35.

I can temporarily switch the Validate task to use the F35 image, then we can just revert that commit later.  Presumably the Fedora people think 1.18 will release before F36 releases?
Force-push: Added [Cirrus: Temporarily validate in F35](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/13376/commits/5612931df321d612a426e51c6a695ff9fb558cb0)
Force-push: Enable `$NETWORK_BACKEND=netavar` in containerized e2e and rootless

[The int podman fedora-36 root container failures](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/6631604939390976/html/int-podman-fedora-36-root-container.log.html) (log) are odd, but on closer inspection seem like more instances of [the issue I opened the other week.](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/13533)
Fresh images + rebased onto https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/13696

@edsantiago maybe you have a better idea how to fix this:  Several tasks are barfing due to not having an identity set.  I think it's due to a new git version in F36.  For example, in the build-each-commit task ([log](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5832146471354368)) but there are/were some others as well.  They all go something like this:

```
/usr/bin/time --verbose --output="$STATS_LOGFILE" $GOSRC/$SCRIPT_BASE/runner.sh
************************************************************
Runner executing altbuild podman-tests as root on fedora-36(fedora-36)
Current environment VM image: fedora-c4786042493468672
************************************************************
Performing alternate build: Build Each Commit
************************************************************
# Validate that all the commits build on top of origin/main^
git rebase origin/main^ -x "hack/make-and-check-size /tmp/make-size-check.jlWmkNY"
Rebasing (1/14)
Committer identity unknown

*** Please tell me who you are.

Run

  git config --global user.email "you@example.com"
  git config --global user.name "Your Name"

to set your account's default identity.
Omit --global to set the identity only in this repository.

fatal: unable to auto-detect email address (got 'root@cirrus-task-5832146471354368.(none)')
make: *** [Makefile:295: build-all-new-commits] Error 128

Exit status: 2
```

I tried to fix this at the point where we clone the repo (([Cirrus: Fix missing git-enforced runtime identity](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/13376/commits/598e4d04dfd39c747bdfc49f64552b9c7aeba3fd))), but it's not working universally.  Do you have any suggestions?

> I tried to fix this at the point where we clone the repo (([Cirrus: Fix missing git-enforced runtime identity](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/13376/commits/598e4d04dfd39c747bdfc49f64552b9c7aeba3fd))), but it's not working universally. Do you have any suggestions?

@cevich If setting HOME is not doing the trick...
```
git config --file /root/.gitconfig --global user.email "TMcTestFace@example.com"
git config --file /root/.gitconfig --global user.name "Testy McTestface"

```

>fatal: unable to auto-detect email address (got 'root@cirrus-task-5832146471354368.(none)')

Is it possible that something has changed in Cirrus-land instead, yielding invalid hostnames? 

1minutetip is down, so I can't play with a VM :-(

1mt is back up. with `git-2.35.1-1.fc36.x86_64` I see no problems:
```console
# git rebase HEAD^^ -x echo
[works fine]
```
Setting `hostname foo`, same result. I can't set `hostname foo.(none)`. I think this is a Cirrus thing, not a git thing.

> yielding invalid hostnames

I think this comes from GCE, they've got control over the network and resolv.conf on the host.  All else being equal, it has to be something in or an interaction with F36 because the problem doesn't happen in F35.
Ya I don't think I can set the hostname, `google-networkservice` will just clobber it next time it DHCPRENEWs.
@jwhonce that worked, thanks.  Though I'm still a bit worried about the hostname `(none)` thing, I'll keep an eye out for that popping up elsewhere.

@jwhonce I ran the APIv2 tests manually to see what output was being lost in the buffer prior to the test timeout:

```
...cut all "ok" messages...
ok 1052 [70-short-names] DELETE images/localhost/bar:latest : status=200
rootdir: /tmp/test-apiv2.tmp.5ZRTaG
1..1052
env CONTAINERS_CONF=/var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/test/apiv2/containers.conf PODMAN=./bin/podman \
        pytest --disable-warnings ./test/apiv2/python
================================ test session starts =================================
platform linux -- Python 3.10.4, pytest-6.2.5, py-1.11.0, pluggy-1.0.0
python client -- requests library
rootdir: /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/podman
plugins: requests-mock-1.8.0
collected 41 items

test/apiv2/python/rest_api/test_v2_0_0_container.py s................          [ 41%]
test/apiv2/python/rest_api/test_v2_0_0_image.py ........                       [ 60%]
test/apiv2/python/rest_api/test_v2_0_0_manifest.py .                           [ 63%]
test/apiv2/python/rest_api/test_v2_0_0_network.py <hang>
```

So it's not the `registry_start()` like was speculated.  However, based on the filename I'm guessing the hang has something to do with this being a netavark envronment (CNI packages & config was erased).  Could I have some help debugging/fixing/working around this please?

@jwhonce update: shit...so I stuck the `--verbose` option onto pytest in the Makefile and the tests pass (manually).  So there's seems to be some kind of race condition :confounded: I just pushed that change to this PR, let's see if the APIv2 tests pass with it...

...ugh, nope still hangs.  I was able to pull this off the VM:

```
[root@packer-624b2ce8-9f97-db25-937e-35f375ec9460 podman]# pstree -p 4930
make(4930)───pytest(5028)───podman(8492)─┬─{podman}(8493)
                                         ├─{podman}(8494)
                                         ├─{podman}(8495)
                                         ├─{podman}(8496)
                                         ├─{podman}(8497)
                                         ├─{podman}(8499)
                                         └─{podman}(8568)
```

The log kept by the cirrus-ci-agent has no useful output beyond what we've already seen.  I wonder if that means something like the log analyzer is causing loss of the buffer when the test times out.  I'll have to test that theory tomorrow.

@edsantiago could you help me with the upgrade tests?  I tried fixing them with 895bfa85aeb511ffd6d3749c8e11150ee97c99b4 but that only makes (all of) them [complain about something else](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/5809378849718272/html/upgrade_test-podman-fedora-36-root-host.log.html) (log) :cry: If it's not a simple cure, I can remove that commit, file an issue and temp. disable the tests until it's fixed.  Otherwise let me know what you think.
@jwhonce and @Luap99 removing `debug` didn't seem to change anything :cry:

```
level=error msg="The storage 'driver' option must be set in /etc/containers/storage.conf, guarantee proper operation."'
```
As best I can tell this is coming from podman on the actual host, not the podman container. Is there a chance that this file is getting corrupted?

> As best I can tell this is coming from podman on the actual host, not the podman container. Is there a chance that this file is getting corrupted?

Sorry I wasn't clear.  I added a commit to `touch` this file on the host, otherwise I get an error about the file not existing (since it's a bind-mount in the podman command run by the test).  The file isn't there by default after `make install`,  somehow it was before :confused: Maybe I broke something, but it seems like as soon as this started running on F36, the upgrade tests broke :confounded:
So you're just doing `touch` to create the file, but not actually putting any content in it? That doesn't seem right.

> So you're just doing touch to create the file, but not actually putting any content in it? That doesn't seem right.

Correct.  I'm not sure where the file went, but somehow it's not there and nothing else complains about it missing :confused:

The setup for the upgrade test is exactly the same as for `int` and `sys`:

```bash
        remove_packaged_podman_files
        make && make install PREFIX=/usr ETCDIR=/etc
```

Looks like `storage.conf` comes from the containers-common package.  Maybe that's where the change is in F36?

(Sorry for the delay; 1minutetip is s-l-o-w right now). I just installed `podman-4.0.3-1.fc37` and `containers-common-1-55.fc37` on what 1mt gave me for an f36 VM. There is no `/etc/containers/storage.conf` even though `rpm -ql containers-common` lists it. Probably a ghost file. Anyhow, I think we need to start by removing that `touch`, i.e., leaving the system with no such file. Everything seems to work just fine without it.

> Anyhow, I think we need to start by removing that touch, i.e., leaving the system with no such file. Everything seems to work just fine without it.

Okay, so then I should also remove the volume-mount from the podman command in the test?  Or maybe make it conditional on the file existing (or not)?  

ref: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/2a882b7703574dc890dd8e3a07be5ca9decd68ac/test/upgrade/test-upgrade.bats#L168

Oh, I get it now! Yes, make it conditional:
```bash
sconf=/etc/containers/storage.conf
v_sconf=
if [[ -e $sconf ]]; then
    v_sconf="-v $sconf:$sconf"
fi
```

@edsantiago so if it isn't one thing, it's another :disappointed: All the upgrade tests are seemingly trying to setup then re-use CNI networks when the host has a netavark setup.  Example: `Error: error configuring network namespace for container c17...387: CNI network "podman" not found`

* [Upgrade test: from v2.1.1](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/4661648563634176/html/upgrade_test-podman-fedora-36-root-host.log.html)
* [Upgrade test: from v3.1.2](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/5787548470476800/html/upgrade_test-podman-fedora-36-root-host.log.html)
* [Upgrade test: from v3.4.4](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/5224598517055488/html/upgrade_test-podman-fedora-36-root-host.log.html)

But I think with F36 & Podman 4, there is no support for "network upgrades" from previous versions, so maybe this is the expected result?  Assuming I'm seeing this right, I'm at a loss of what to do :confused:
For purposes of upgrade test, the only meaningful config is to set up CNI on the parent system. @Luap99 what's the cleanest way to do that?

> set up CNI on the parent system

This may be possible actually, we make the "Use Netavark" commitment in `setup_environment.sh`.  So I think maybe I just need to mess with the logic so we do the reverse when `$TEST_FLAVOR=upgrade`.  In other words, remove netavark & aardvark-dns packages, and ensure the CNI directory and config is present.  That will get kind of messy, but maybe I can pull it off.

Try with only adding containernetworking-plugins first and not removing netavark because this is the common scenario when updating on fedora and thus should be tested. You also have to install the cni config file since this is no longer installed by the podman package.
`mv ./cni/87-podman-bridge.conflist /etc/cni/net.d/` should work, of course this must be conditional and check if either `87-podman-bridge.conflist` or `87-podman.conflist` already exist before overwriting user configuration. (DO NOT USE `podman network ls` to check for the default network!)
Thanks for the tips @Luap99
[***Ref.: first 99% successful run***](https://cirrus-ci.com/build/5458443447304192) prior to branching-off dependent commits into dedicated PRs for review.
/hold
@cevich rebase is your life with this PR....

> rebase is your life with this PR....

...and again every six-months with a Fedora update :sweat:

This is way too complicated for a thorough review right now, so I'm only addressing the new git behavior. My understanding from a quick ddg search is, if (git barfs unsafe repository), then (git config --global --add safe.directory PATH). I do not see the string `config` in your diffs. Have you tried doing that? 

(Speaking simply as someone who only cares about getting things working again, not if the solution is perfect)
One question that could be addressed later.  Otherwise LGTM.  @edsantiago if you could take a deep dive, I'd appreciate it.
force-push: Rebased, and tweaked two comments.
@cevich is this ready for review?

> @cevich is this ready for review?

Yes please.

Edit: If you think it will make reviewing easier, I can probably split off the [Cirrus: Fix ownership of repos. to keep git happy](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/13376/commits/9c0c29f0861fea4ac3c76e6c1314e2b44582f3ab) commit into a separate PR.  The other 5 commits need to stay here though.

LGTM with one question
@baude @edsantiago PTAL

/approve
/lgtm

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

I have quibbles, but nothing that warrants yet another re-push on this two-month saga. Thanks for your hard work on this @cevich 

/hold cancel
Should probably start working on f37 now.  :^(
New Cirrus task map now on [wiki](https://github.com/containers/podman/wiki/Cirrus-Dependency-Visualization)

> Should probably start working on f37 now. :^(

Thanks for the help reviewing and fixing all the issues this raised along the way.  This one was especially difficult due to the netavark/aardvark switchup.  Though, now we can all sleep better at night, knowing the test-coverage of the new networking stack has been vastly expanded.

Next up is actually Ubuntu 22.04 :cry:
